workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you extract the pipeline hygiene from this email thread for Treyarch Global? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 11:56 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 09:12 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 14:39 EST
Subject: Follow-up: pipeline hygiene Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-L trap (Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-L failure mode (Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-L -- Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
